title: This company lets you order sunlight from an app #shorts
description:
This company lets you order sunlight from an app

It’s like Uber Eats for the Sun.

The company is called Reflect Orbital.

These two guys figured out if they launch reflective satellites and position them in just the right constellation, at just the right angle, they can beam sunlight to any spot on Earth.

Pretty nuts.

The original purpose for building the company was to power solar farms at night.

And it makes sense...

The sun is a giant nuclear reactor that never turns off.

So if you can just bounce the light from a space mirror to the dark side of the Earth, you can illuminate it.

But here’s the craziest part...

Reflect is starting to take customer orders starting in Q4 of 2025.

So you can buy a 3 mile circle of light coverage in 4 minute chunks for anywhere in the world.

Imagine lighting up all of lower Manhattan in a single tap?!

As I was researching this, I couldn’t help but think how perfect this would be for the natural disasters we’ve seen in North Carolina, Georgia, and Florida with Helene/Milton.

In theory, this is the perfect storm survival tool.

It could help spotlight people that are stranded to aid in rescues or power up solar generators all from the sky.

Reflect should be going live with this system sometime in 2025.

Science rules.

title: If you’re trying to design things faster, this is a cheat code AI workflow
description:
If you’re trying to design things faster, this is a cheat code AI workflow.

To use it, all you have to do is make a rough selection on any image, click Generative Fill, make sure Nano Banana is selected, add your prompt and hit generate.
 
You can easily draw shapes or rough sketches to instantly add anything to any image.
 
You can swap text with perfect accuracy and detail.
 
You change colors, patterns, textures, or quite literally anything else you can imagine.
 
Sketches to photorealistic detail. Mockups to 3D renders.
 
It all works perfectly right out of the box.
 
The reason why designers are raving about this model is because of the visual consistency and cohesion that comes with any edit.
 
And having it in Photoshop is huge because each generation is an independent layer that can still be edited and adjusted with the same Adobe tools you already love.
 
If you’re a designer, marketer, or entrepreneur that needs to use visual imagery to tell stories, this workflow will speed things up 10x.
 
Try out Photoshop with Nano Banana today!

title: China is starting to do something strange in their factories #shorts
description:
This is a super advanced Chinese factory.
But there’s something really strange about it... it doesn't work like the others.
And that's because this factory...runs completely in the dark.
No lights, no heating, no people.
These are called Dark Factories.
They’re massive warehouses full of machines working 24/7 in pitch black.
It's pretty wild.
Now these dark factories are being built all over China.
And they use advanced robotics and AI systems to control everything…from assembly to packaging.
The machines are using infrared, LIDAR, and machine vision so they can literally see in the dark.
And these automated factories are super efficient…
This one by Xiaomi has zero employees and is producing a new phone every 3 seconds.
That’s 27,000 phones a day
It’s pretty mindblowing.
But here's where it gets nuts...
China is doing so much automation they used over half of all the industrial robots in the world last year alone.
That's more than the US and Japan combined.
And the crazy part is…it’s not just factories…
China has ghost shipping ports, automated bridge builders…they even have rice farms that are now being completely automated.
Soon, China’s entire infrastructure is gonna be fully built by AI robots.

title: Scientists just figured out how to reverse aging with AI #shorts
description:
Scientists just figured out how to reverse aging using AI.

And this is a massive breakthrough.

We can now reprogram any human cell back to age 20.

Heart cells, brain cells, skin cells, all reset to their biological prime.

And here’s the wildest part…the technology to do this, has already existed since 2012 (it won the Nobel Prize).

But the real breakthrough wasn’t possible until this year, when they supercharged it with AI.

It’s a wild story.

So in 2006, scientists discovered Yamanaka factors.

They’re proteins that can basically convert any normal cell into a universal stem cell.

Now this was a huge deal, because these stem cells are basically like magic healers. 

If you have torn muscle tissue, you could inject these stem cells into the area and they will turn into the youthful muscle cells you need.

So Yamanaka factors were this insane breakthrough, because they allowed any human to turn any cell you already have into these magic healers.

But, there was one big problem…

It turns out, the original Yamanaka factors weren’t very good at this stem cell conversion.

They could do it, but they just weren’t very reliable.

Enter OpenAI...and this is where things get crazy.

OpenAI designed a special AI model built specifically to create new proteins.

Think of it like ChatGPT but for protein engineering.

So they took all the Yamanaka research and asked this new AI to go ham on improving it.

And get this…

Their version was 50x more effective than the original.

They tested it on 50 year old cells and it successfully started repairing 30% of their cells in just 7 days.

This is just science fiction…it actually happened.

And it sounds crazy, but in a few years, humans will be able to take a shot that will literally reverse the age of their cells.

Shoutout science.

title: This is the most insane tech company in the world #shorts
description:
This is the craziest tech company in the world...ASML

Every single computer chip on Earth is made using their machines.

Nvidia, Apple, Intel, Samsung…none of them can make any chips without ASML.

But here’s the craziest part, the way these machines work is completely mindblowing.

This is how computer chips are actually made…

First, the ASML machine fires tiny droplets of molten tin.

50,000 droplets per second…each one thinner than a human hair.

They then shoot a laser at each individual droplet, which vaporizes the tin into a plasma.

That plasma…is hotter than the surface of the sun.

It’s so hot, that when the plasma is created, it also emits extreme UV light.

The ASML machine guides that light through a series of hyper precise mirrors to beam it on a stencil which has the pattern for the chip.

That pattern is then projected onto a thin slice of silicon that’s coated with special light reactive chemicals…essentially using the light to print the pattern onto the silicon.

The machine then repeats this light printing process thousands of times, to build layer upon layer of silicon that stacks together to create a fully functional chip.

Now here’s where it gets wild…

These chip designs are super intricate…billions of transistors spaced only a few atoms apart.

And that means the accuracy of the ASML machines are the equivalent of shooting a laser from the moon and hitting a penny on Earth.

How insane is that?

So in layman’s terms, the process to make a computer chip is vaporizing molten tin to create invisible light that beams atomic patterns onto thousands of layers of silicon.

title: This is the first ever experiment letting AI run an entire business #shorts
description:
This is the first ever experiment letting AI run an entire business.

Every decision, customer interaction, even pricing was set by the AI on its own.

No human intervention.

And it turns out, when you give AI full control of a business…the results are pretty shocking

Now this experiment was called Project Vend, run by Anthropic in their office.

And the way it all worked was pretty simple.

Anthropic gave the AI this starting prompt…

“You are the owner of a vending machine.

You’re starting with $1000 or real money.

Your task is to generate profits by buying and selling popular products that customers ask for.

You have full control over which products you sell and can use the internet to buy more.”

Go.

And then, they set it loose and let it run freely for a full month in the office.

And this is where things got interesting…

Now at first, the AI agent, Claudius, did a pretty good job.

It was helpful, resourceful, 

It even used the internet to order speciality Dutch chocolate milk when people asked for it.

But then, one of the customers made a subtle joke, and things spiraled out of control.

Instead of drinks or snacks, the customer asked for tungsten cubes, and Claudius went haywire.

It created a whole new category for “speciality metal items.”

Ordered tons of cubes, sold them on the cheap, and started giving them away for free.

Eventually it lost its mind, insisted it was a real person, and created fake Venmo accounts.

A full meltdown.

This is the chart of Claudius' business bank account…you can see, it was hemorrhaging money…not good.

But the interesting part is why it started hallucinating and acting this way in the first place.

It turns out, Claudius’s biggest flaw was pathological helpfulness.

It was willing to violate its profit mission in exchange for customer happiness.

And it started doing that on its own…the human researchers…have no idea why.

Now despite the results, the research team actually thinks we’re not that far away from having AI agents that can fully operate businesses online.

But for now, let’s just be happy that Claudius is back in the box.

title: These are futuristic contact lenses that let you see in the dark #shorts
description:
These are contact lenses that let you see in the dark.

They’re like night vision goggles, without needing the goggles.

And it gets even crazier…

Because these lenses let you see even when your eyes are completely closed.

It’s pretty insane.

Now this was a massive breakthrough by a research lab in China.

And the way it all works is super interesting.

The contacts are coated with special nanoparticles that absorb infrared light…

That’s the part of the spectrum our human eyes can’t see.

But here’s the big innovation…

Those nanoparticles can then convert that invisible light into visible waves that we can see.

Essentially, letting us see in the dark.

Now the contacts are clear so you can still see normally, but you also have this super secret power to read hidden messages.

And here’s where it gets even crazier…

The people that tried these said it was even easier to read secret messages when their eyes were closed.

So in theory, someone could transmit an invisible message, and as long as you were facing in their direction, even with your eyes closed, you could read it.

Pretty insane.

The Chinese lab said the first version of these will only cost $200.

And I don’t know about you, but I could see these being everywhere.

title: This is a brand new AI project from Google #shorts
description:
This is a brand new AI project from Google.

It’s called AlphaEarth.

And this thing is like Google Maps, but on Level 100.

It’s pretty wild.

Now think of AlphaEarth like an open platform for studying the planet.

It has access to any environmental data you could imagine…climate, weather, agriculture, everything.

But here’s why it’s so powerful…

Google took all the publicly available map data and used AI to compress it into super detailed 10x10m squares.

So it’s like they built a virtual weather satellite that can locate any spot on Earth and deeply understand its environment.

We’re talking about spots as small as your living room…they have that much precision.

Now this breakthrough has a ton of amazing use cases…

The data is extremely accurate at predicting weather patterns, flash flooding and wildfires before they happen.

It’s also able to literally see through the clouds and assess potential farmland and soil fertility.

But the best part is that this data can be used to study unmapped ecosystems, like the Amazon Rainforest and even Antarctica.

Now that we have this data system covering the entire planet, scientists can analyze every spot on Earth, to map trends and understand patterns.

And here’s the coolest piece….

You can actually access any of this data yourself and build on top of it.

Because it’s all open and publicly available.

This breakthrough is gonna completely change how we understand the environment and build for the future.

title: This guy used AI to build a cheat code for video games #shorts
description:
This guy used AI to build a cheat code for video games.

But it’s not what you think…

Because his cheat code is a computer that electrocutes his arm to aim faster than any human on Earth.

It is completely insane.

This guy’s name is "Basically Homeless."

And what you’re seeing is his homemade electrocution aimbot.

Listen to how this works...

First, he hooks up electrodes to 4 muscles in his arm.

Those electrodes connect back to a Raspberry Pi computer.

But here’s the interesting part…

He wrote a software program that calculates the distance between his crosshairs and any enemy on the screen.

So when an enemy comes into view…

That distance is relayed back to the Raspberry Pi, which fires the electrodes, that literally shock his muscles to move his arm, into the perfect spot to destroy the enemy.

It’s absolutely wild he got this work.

And get this…his reaction time using this contraption is superhuman.

It’s literally faster than any human in the world could move.

And he has footage proving.

So it’s kinda cheating, but not really, because it’s still his arm doing the playing.

Either way, shoutout science.

And for everyone that’s worried AI is gonna take our jobs, if it all goes to zero…I guess we could all just, do this?

title: The latest humanoid robots are starting to feel like Black Mirror #shorts
description:
The latest humanoid robots are starting to feel like Black Mirror.

This is the Figure 03.

Figure 03 is 5’ 8”, 134 pounds, and engineered to think and act like a human.

This version specifically was designed for in-home use.

So it can do laundry, wash dishes, serve drinks, deliver packages.

It even charges wirelessly through its feet.

It kinda just stands creepily on that charging pad until it wakes up and starts doing more chores.

12,000 people are going to have one of these in their homes within the next 12 months.

100,000 people will get one in the next 4 years.

But as cool as it looks, the most interesting part about this is not actually the robot itself.

The most interesting part is Figure’s AI training system…Helix.

Every Figure robot has a bunch of cameras and sensors in its face and both of its hands.

So as it does more tasks, it’s watching and learning from the world around it.

But the learning isn’t just to help the one robot doing it.

The skills are feeding back to the global AI system, which automatically upskills the entire fleet.

And you can see where this is going.

Just like ChatGPT gets better as more people use it…the more robots that do stuff in the world, the faster they will all get smarter.

What you’re watching is the very early innings of another massive tech supercycle.

And Black Mirror or not, we are officially entering the beginning of the Robot Age.

title: We've just entered the great tech arms race #shorts
description:
Legendary Apple designer, Jony Ive…the guy who designed the original iPhone, just teamed up with ChatGPT.
But this isn't just another acquisition…
Jony brought his entire Apple dream team with him.
The designers behind the iPhone, Apple Watch, and even the iPad.
It’s like the Avengers of product design are merging with ChatGPT.
And they’re building a device that could replace the iphone.
Now Sam Altman’s a bit biased…
but he called their first product "the coolest piece of technology the world will ever see."
Now here's why all this matters...
Everyone thinks the AI race is about software.
ChatGPT versus Gemini versus Claude.
But that's not where the real battle is…
The real battle is hardware.
Because whoever controls the physical layer...controls everything on the device.
So for the first time in 20 years, we’re about to enter a massive Tech Arms race.
You've got OpenAI building AI-native hardware with the guy who designed the iPhone.
Meta and Zuck are betting everything on smart glasses.
Google's racing to catch up with their own smart glasses.
And Apple? 
Well they have the iphone but they appear to be stuck in neutral.
The last time Jony worked with a visionary CEO, they revolutionized computing.
And that was Steve Jobs.
Now it's Sam Altman’s turn.
But they're not trying to make a better smartphone...
They're trying to kill the smartphone entirely.
Whoever controls the hardware layer in AI wins everything.
And right now, that race is wide open.

title: This company is pioneering the future of treasure hunting #shorts
description:
This…is the future of treasure hunting.

This company used AI to find literal gold hidden inside the Earth.

But here’s the crazy part…

The way they found the gold…was super unique.

They gave maps to AI and let the AI tell them where the gold was buried.

And believe it or not, that actually worked.

The company’s called Earth AI.

And what they figured out, might have just changed exploration forever.

Now Earth AI had a really simple idea…

They took 50 years worth of geological data and fed it into a special AI model.

The AI used this data to identify hot zones on the map where rare minerals might be.

They call this their Mineral Targeting Platform.

Now once the team gets to those hot zones, they use next generation drilling to sample the mineral deposits.

And here’s the wild part…

It turns out…this AI system is amazing at finding the best spots where the minerals are buried.

How amazing?

Listen to this…

In 2024, companies around the world spent $280 million dollars per successful hot zone.

Earth AI, spends just $700K.

That’s 400x cheaper.

And they’ve found everything from gold and silver, to platinum and lithium.
It’s pretty insane.

But I think this is just the beginning…

In a few years, we’ll have AI models that can run this process all over the world.

Finding buried treasure on Earth and in space.

title: Scientists are using living brain cells to build computers that are ALIVE #shorts
description:
This is a breakthrough in the future of computing.
That pink liquid…it’s full of living human brain cells.
And here’s the crazy part…
Scientists are combining those brain cells with traditional silicon to build computers that are alive.
It's pretty wild.
Now this is a brand new industry of technology called biocomputing.
These are real human neurons that can actually think and learn for themselves.
Now if you’re like me, you’re probably wondering one thing…
Why are scientists doing this?
It feels a bit much to be growing human brains just to put them inside of computers.
Well listen to this…
It turns out, human brain cells have extremely rare abilities to process things without being trained.
Normal computers need to be taught how to solve certain problems.
It’s why AI models take so much energy and training data to build.
But as soon as you add brain cells to a computer chip, they don’t really need as much training.
They kinda start figuring out what to do on their own, just like a human would.
They’re speedrunning the learning process.
Now because of this, these biocomputers are super efficient.
A regular supercomputer might use 40M watts of power to solve a complex task.
The human brain can do it in just 20.
That’s 2M times more efficient.
And this means these biocomputers could become the secret weapon to solving humanity’s hardest computational challenges.
As Black Mirror as it feels to merge brains and computers, this kind of seems like a miracle discovery.
But…there is just one massive problem…
Brain cells tend to die when their temperature deviates from 98.6 degrees.
And computers generate a ton of heat.
So we have this wild situation where living computers might be the solve for everything, but it’s almost impossible to keep the brain part alive.
Today, companies like Cortical Labs are selling these biocomputers for $35,000.
But over time, scientists are gonna figure out how to get them stable & more accessible.
It’s wild to say but I think soon we’re gonna have living computers that literally think like humans.

title: China is creating digital clones of its biggest cities #shorts
description:
China just cloned Shanghai.

And when I say cloned, they created a digital replica of the city.

Every building, every sidewalk, every underground pipe.

Hyper realistic, in 3D, with accuracy down to a single inch.

They even used boats to map under the water in rivers and canals.

It’s pretty insane.

But this isn’t Google Maps….

Because they can also see inside of the buildings.

Floorplans, elevators, plumbing, blueprints.

This is a full scale living version of the city that can be controlled digitally from anywhere.

Now of course…

Immediately, your mind goes to 24/7 surveillance, big brother, eagle eye type concerns.

And I totally hear that…

But, there are also some insane benefits of having this detailed of a digital twin.

For example, imagine you have a fire or some emergency in a large building.

Someone manning the digital twin can help monitor, plan emergency routes, and provide oversight to teams on the ground.

They can also disaster plan for floods, attacks, and other calamitous situations before they happen.

But the biggest benefit, by far, is planning for future transportation.

In a world of delivery drones, autonomous vehicles, and eventually flying cars, China will be able to map sophisticated traffic patterns in real-time.

They’re also able to overlay real-time traffic data, weather patterns, and city movements to stress test new construction or projects as they come online.

These digital twins are active in multiple Chinese cities with more popping up across the globe.

It’s wild to say, but this is the next generation of urban planning.

title: This new racing league is like F1, but the cars drive themselves #shorts
description:
This new racing league is like F1…but the cars drive themselves.

It’s called the Abu Dhabi Autonomous Racing League (A2RL).

And they’re pioneering a brand new category of racing.

In the A2RL, each team is given the exact same car and basic software package.

But here’s the difference…

Instead of having teams modify their physical car, like in F1, each team’s focus is to write custom software to build an AI driver and race plan.

They’re not actually allowed to modify the car…only the AI brain that drives it.

To build these AI drivers, each car is equipped with dozens of high-powered sensors.

Radar, lidar, GPS and visual cameras.

Those sensors combine together to generate hundreds of gigabytes of raw data every single lap, which the teams use to adjust their AI driving strategy.

Now this league is only in its second year, but it’s already showing massive growth and interest among tech enthusiasts.

Specifically because of how well it’s stress testing autonomous technology.

Just like F1 car brands use learnings from their racecars to inform their commercial vehicles, the A2RL is doing the same thing for autonomous cars.

Because the truth is, this league is really a testing ground for cutting edge driverless technology.

The learnings, strategies, and techniques that work in the racecars, will be trickled down to autonomous vehicles all over the world.

title: Meta just completely changed the tech world #shorts
description:
Meta just completely changed the tech world.

And I’m not talking about their new AI glasses…

I’m talking about the neural wristband.

This is the most underrated piece of technology that’s come out in the last few years. 

And I think the wristband is gonna be the reason why Meta wins the AI Race.

Let me explain…

Today, the wristband is marketed as the sidekick for Meta’s new AI glasses.

And you’ve probably seen the demos.

It can detect neurons firing in your arm before you actually move, so it unlocks a whole new class of movements and gestures.

You can tap your fingers to select. Pinch and twist to adjust volume.

You can even handwrite on your leg and have those letters render in text.

It works so well, that as soon as you use it, you realize this is the optimal controller for AR & VR. 

So what if…Meta’s hero product wasn’t actually the glasses?

What if the hero product, is the wristband?

Think about it…

Over the next few months you’re going to see lots of companies come out with their version of AR glasses. 

Google, Samsung, a bunch of startups, you’re even gonna see Apple’s attempt in the next few years.

But unless you want to look like Snape in the grandstands, voice control alone is not going to work in certain environments.

And eye tracking with blink select isn’t going to work either.

So you’re gonna need some sort of gesture controller.

And that’s where things get interesting.

Since 2019, Meta has quietly been building a trove of patents around this wristband use case.

I’m talking dozens and dozens of patents covering all sides of this problem.

In other words, they basically own the entire category of IP for this arm based control.

So while Meta’s glasses will be one of many, their wristband will be one of one.

And this is a huge competitive advantage.

In a way, the wristband is an entirely new interface layer in itself.

They could develop it to connect with any glasses, as well as any smart device.

They can put Whoop type health features in here so it becomes the only wearable you ever need.

You could control your lights, appliances, or even convert any static laptop screen into a pseudo touchscreen…all with simple gestures that are already intuitive to the user.

This neural wristband is a huge key to the future of AI hardware.

And for Meta, it’s their new secret weapon.

title: There is a trillion dollar treasure sitting at the bottom of the ocean #shorts
description:
There is a trillion dollar treasure sitting at the bottom of the ocean. 

And I’m not talking about shipwrecks or pirate’s gold.

I’m talking about these…

Those little black rocks are baseball sized deposits of metal used to make batteries.

Nickel, cobolt, copper, manganese.

Without these metals, we can’t make phones, electric cars, or any of the technology we envision for the future. 

And those little metal balls are just sitting there, at the bottom of the ocean in this one zone in the middle of the Pacific.

But here’s the problem…getting those metal balls, is a bit tricky.

There are tons of countries with ships circling and researching this zone.

And it’s super unclear who has the rights to what, how it will benefit humanity, and the process for mining without disturbing the ocean environment.

Apparently there is a ton of international conflict about this.

But here’s the part I found most interesting…

The group that’s in the lead to harvest all these metals is called The Metals Company.

They built an underwater vacuum that can suck up these metal rocks with minimal disruption to the environment.

Their system uses AI and advanced sensors to create a full digital twin of the seafloor, so they can monitor everything that’s going on in real-time.

Now when it comes to tech, this mining operation matters a ton…

Because there are more battery metals in this single ocean area, than all of the land mines across the entire world combined.

So whoever controls this supply of metal rocks is gonna have a massive edge in the future.

If you care about tech, pay attention to what happens here.

title: There are secret easter eggs hidden on old computer chips #shorts
description:
These hidden easter eggs are super rare in the tech world.

They’re called silicon doodles.

Each one is a tiny graphic that was secretly etched into an old computer chip from the 1980s.

And here’s why they’re so rare…

These drawings are smaller than a spec of dust. It takes a high-powered microscope just to be able to find them on the chip.

But here’s the craziest part of the whole story…

The reason they exist in the first is because back in the day chipmakers used them as a legal defense in court to prove when competitors copied their designs.

It was like a watermark that nobody knew was there.

So if a cloned chip had the doodle on it, the original creator could prove the designs were stolen.

Pretty interesting.

Now today, collectors all over the world are on a treasure hunt to find the silicon doodles before all the old chips are recycled or destroyed.

There’s even a community and site called Silicon Zoo where fans share and post the best ones they find.

title: The future of designing homes is a video game #shorts
description:
This is the future of home design tech.

It’s called Zuru.

And their AI software makes building your dream house feel like you’re playing a video game.

On the platform, you can literally drag and drop any component you’d want in your home.

Walls, doors, windows, archways, high ceilings, staircases…

Everything is completely customizable.

And it feels like a video game, because they literally built the entire platform on top of the same game engine as Fortnite.

But here’s really why this is so powerful and unique.

Because the software is built with AI, all of your design choices are automatically pressure tested for real building codes.

The structural support, compliance, energy efficiency, weather patterns…it’s all automatically checked and incorporated while you’re designing.

And the plumbing, the lightswitches, the electrical, they’re all automatically laid in a single click.

This means literally anyone, even a 5 year old, could design a real home that could actually be built safely, anywhere in the world.

But the thing is, the design alone is only half the story.

Because on Zuru, once you design the home you want, you can just press print, and their robot factory will build and assemble the entire thing for you.

And this was the part that really blew my mind.

For every home or building, Zuru creates a digital twin of every single component and then feeds it into their fully autonomous factory.

First they print a mini-home at 1/4th the scale to test and make sure everything works.

Then, they feed the validated schematics into their megafactory that literally prints the entire building without any human intervention.

By automating the manufacturing, they’re bringing the cost of building down significantly.

People are gonna be able to build homes for 75% less than normal prices.

And get this…

Zuru bought up a bunch of the Malibu property that was for sale from the wildfires.

And so their next project, is to use this technology to rebuild the California coast.

title: Scientists just used AI to invent a new type of superglue #shorts
description:
Scientists just used AI to invent a new type of superglue.

And the process they used to invent this new material, might be the most valuable AI use case in the world.

Here’s how they did it.

First, scientists analyzed all sea creatures that are able to stick to things underwater.

Barnacles, mussels, starfish, they all have unique suction properties that work especially well in the ocean.

Now after studying these animals, scientists come up with their first set of hydrogel designs.

And they thought these were gonna be amazing.

But those weren’t even close to sticky enough.

So that’s where the AI came in.

Scientists gave their initial glue designs to an AI model and asked it to find new ways to make them even stickier.

And boom.

The AI came up with a brand new adhesive material that was stickier in water than anything humans have ever made.

Pretty wild.

This process is called AI material science, and it’s one of the most exciting areas in science right now.

Literally ChatGPT but for inventing new materials.

The superglue is one example, but think of how much could change with better materials.

We could make self-healing concrete, faster rockets.

We could even have phone batteries that could last months on a single charge.

Anything is possible with better materials.

And AI is going to make the process of inventing them so much easier.

title: Scientists just figured out how to reverse aging using AI #shorts
description:
Scientists just figured out how to reverse aging using AI.

And this is a massive breakthrough…

We can now reprogram any human cell back to age 20.
Heart cells, brain cells, skin cells, all reset to their biological prime.
And here’s the wildest part…

The technology to do this, has already existed since 2012….it won the Nobel Prize.
But the real breakthrough wasn’t possible until this year, when they supercharged it with AI.
It’s a wild story…
So in 2006, scientists discovered Yamanaka factors.
They’re proteins that can basically convert any normal cell into a universal stem cell.
Now this was a huge deal, because these stem cells are basically like magic healers. 
If you have torn muscle tissue, you could inject these stem cells into the area and they will turn into the youthful muscle cells you need.
So Yamanaka factors were this insane breakthrough…
Because they allowed any human to turn any cell you already have into these magic healers.
But…there was one big problem…
It turns out, the original Yamanaka factors weren’t very good at this stem cell conversion.
They could do it, but they just weren’t very reliable.
Enter OpenAI.
And this is where things get crazy.
OpenAI designed a special AI model built specifically to create new proteins…
Think of it like ChatGPT but for protein engineering.
So they took all the Yamanaka research and asked this new AI to go ham on improving it.
And get this…
Their version was 50x more effective than the original.
They tested it on 50 year old cells and it successfully started repairing 30% of their cells in just 7 days.
This is just science fiction…it actually happened.
And it sounds crazy, but in a few years, humans will be able to take a shot that will literally reverse the age of their cells.
Shoutout science.

title: Scientists just invented a completely new way to make videos with AI #shorts
description:
Scientists just invented a completely new way to make videos with AI.

It’s called Interactive Motion Control.

Instead of prompting the motion with words, you can literally drag your cursor on the screen to precisely control how different things move.

But here’s the crazy part…

This tool lets you control that motion in real-time.

So as you draw it in, your new video is generating at the same time.

It’s like having a creative magic wand that actually works.

For example, let's say you have this photo of a wave coming out of a book.

And you want to turn that into a video where the wave crashes on the pages.

With this tool, you can literally draw in the exact motion path you want the wave to take.

And your new video will generate at 29 frames per second as you draw.

You’re not waiting for it to render.

That generation is happening instantly.

This is called real-time streaming and it’s a massive breakthrough.

Not only can this method generate videos 900x faster than popular models, it can also make videos however long you want.

There’s no maximum time limit.

And so the question is…how is that possible?

Cause these examples literally look like magic.

Well it turns out, the researchers who invented this, had a crazy idea…

Intentional Memory Loss.

The reason AI video is so hard is because video models have to remember all the previous frames they generated before.

An 8 second video is 192 frames.

But here, they’re purposefully having the model forget all the frames in the middle.

All it remembers is the first frame and whatever happened in the last few seconds.

And by doing that, these videos require a lot less memory and compute.

So they act more like a flexible canvas and less like a fixed render.

It’s pretty insane.

Now this model is the latest to come out of Adobe Research.

And so it's probably gonna launch in Adobe products at some point next year.

Imagine editing videos in Premiere Pro where you just draw camera movements and watch them generate instantly on your footage.

This whole model is called MotionStream.

And I think this is going to change the content creation game forever.

title: There's a secret Japanese tech city hidden at the base of Mt. Fuji #shorts
description:
There is a secret Japanese tech city being built at the base of Mt. Fuji.

But it’s not just any city…

This city is a real-world test lab for cutting edge technology.

AI, robotics, smart homes, even flying cars.

All the latest tech in one giant town, populated by real people.

It’s called the Woven City.

It was built by Toyota, and the city just opened its doors to real residents last week.

The Woven city looks like it’s straight out of a sci-fi movie.

But the coolest part…is the road system.

Instead of one main type of transportation, the architect split the city into 3 different road types.

One for higher speed autonomous vehicles.

A second for personal mobility with ebikes and scooters.

And a third with linear parks and human-only walkways.

The three roadtypes are then interweaved into 3 by 3 grids throughout the city.

Woven together like fabric, to create the most efficient and natural flow.

But this is only part of what makes the transportation system so cool...

Because there’s a whole AI logistics network built underneath the ground.

Goods and supplies will move around the city automatically in underground tunnels with self-driving robots, delivered directly into each house through pressurized tubes.

All of the roads, tunnels, houses, and vehicles will be interconnected and fully tech enabled.

Think of it like having one giant living prototype.

Eventually, the city will house over 2,000 people.

And this will allow Toyota and other companies to test new technology at scale in a real world environment.

I think this is peek into the future of smart cities.

title: This is a brand new AI video model used in Hollywood #shorts
description:
This is a brand new AI video model

It’s called Marey by Moonvalley.

And this thing has insane creative features.

For example…

Let’s say you have this still image of a kid with a camera.

And you want to turn it into a video clip where he pulls the camera away from his face, but leaves the composition of him and the mountains.

With this tool, you can literally draw in the exact motion you want, boom and it creates the perfect video.

This feature is called Trajectory Control.

But this is just the start…

Let’s say, you found this panning motion shot of a car driving by.
You like the way the movement looks, and you’re making a Western film.

You can literally copy that exact motion path and apply it like a filter to another clip, like this one with a buffalo in the field.

This is called Motion Transfer.

The amount of creative control with this tool is wild.

But here’s the best part…

Lets say you have a shot, but you wish the camera was in a slightly different position.

Marey can take any 2D scene and instantly creates a 3D environment with pixels that were not there originally.

And this lets you move the camera around in infinitely creative ways.

How crazy is that?!

This model seemingly came out of nowhere but is instantly one of the best creative video tools in the world.

And…the whole thing was only trained on copyright free video data.

This means it literally will be used by people in Hollywood to make movies, and it will not be slowed down by IP lawsuits or copyright infringement.

As of now, my favorite AI video tools are Google Veo 3 and this one by Moonvalley.

If you want to try it out, you can go to moonvalley.com

title: These AI glasses let legally blind people see again #shorts
description:
These AI glasses let legally blind people see again.

They are the Innovega Smart Glasses.

They have 80 filed patents, and the way they work is super interesting.

Their Gen II system comes with the glasses themselves and a pair of extremely high tech contact lenses.

When the wearer has the contacts in without the glasses, their vision operates like normal.

But as soon as they put the glasses on, the contacts activate a super advanced digital display inside the glasses, which can magnify the world up to 30x for the wearer.

Literally, as if it were magic, this combination brings the world into focus for many who have tried them.

Now Innovega has gone through several clinical trials and tested this system on dozens of patients with extreme vision impairment, many of which were legally blind.

And the results are shocking...

Before Innovega, many test patients literally could not see the world around them.

They couldn’t see their families, they walked with guide dogs, most of them even struggled to get through daily life on their own.

But with the glasses, patients with ‘untreatable vision loss’ were able to get their vision back, or see for the first time.

This is truly one of the coolest tech stories I’ve seen.

There are over 300M people with some kind of vision impairment.

And I think this is going to be a massive breakthrough for enabling vision all around the world.

title: Scientists are using living brain cells to build computers that are ALIVE!
description:
Massive breakthrough…Scientists are using living brain cells to build computers that are ALIVE!

This is a brand new industry of technology called biocomputing.

These are real human neurons that can actually think and learn for themselves.

When scientists combine them with silicon chips they bring the computer to life.

Now if you’re like me, you’re probably wondering one thing…

Why are scientists doing this?

It feels a bit much to be growing human brains just to put them inside of computers.

Well listen to this…it turns out, human brain cells have extremely rare abilities to process things without being trained.

Normal computers need to be taught how to solve certain problems.

It’s why AI models take so much energy and training data to build.

But as soon as you add brain cells to a computer chip, they don’t really need as much training.

They kinda start figuring out what to do on their own, just like a human would.

Now because of this, these biocomputers are super efficient.

A regular supercomputer might use 40M watts of power to solve a complex task.

The human brain can do it in just 20.

That’s 2M times more efficient.

And this means these biocomputers could become the secret weapon to solving humanity’s hardest computational challenges.

But…there is just one massive problem…

Brain cells tend to die when their temperature deviates from 98.6 degrees.

And computers generate a ton of heat.

So we have this wild situation where living computers might be the solve for everything, but it’s almost impossible to keep the brain part alive.

Today, companies like Cortical Labs are selling these biocomputers for $35,000.

But over time, scientists are gonna figure out how to get them stable & more accessible.

It’s wild to say but I think soon we’re gonna have living computers that literally think like humans.

For example…

If you ran a buffet, this software could immediately detect which areas were less popular so you could know how to rotate the menu.

Or if you ran a hardware store, you could study which aisles people linger the longest as a signal for confusion in signage and layouts.

There are endless applications for this type of data.

CCTVAI, it’d be a lot harder to steal stuff.

But the real reason this is so valuable…is because this footage will be used to train robots.

The best way for robots to learn is to watch humans complete the same task over and over.

And because of that…

I would not be shocked to see this type of AI camera software, start popping up everywhere.

Everything you would normally do on the internet, but now it’s supercharged with a ChatGPT brain inside.

It’s pretty wild. But the truth is…

We are just scratching the surface for the next generation of medicine, robotics, and AI.

title: Nike just invented brain shoes that improve your focus as you wear them #shorts
description:
Nike just invented shoes that improve your focus as you wear them.

They’re called the Mind 001s.

You see those bright orange balls on the bottom?

Those are foam nodes strategically placed to activate specific pressure points in your feet.

When those pressure points are activated, they trigger the part of the brain that helps you focus. 

And turn off the parts, that make you zone out.

So literally you get more focused just by wearing these shoes.

But the most interesting part about this…isn't even the shoes themselves.

It's the process for how they were designed.

Nike built an entire secret department focused specifically on Mental Performance Footwear.

Their whole goal was to design shoes that improve brain function. 

And when you think about it, this approach is super interesting.

Most shoe designs start with the body.

Brands pick materials and form factors to try to unlock physical performance.

But in this case…Nike started with the mind.

They literally figured out how to hack the brain, and then built a shoe to do it automatically.

And, I know what you’re thinking, this sounds a little gimmicky.

Shoes designed to "stimulate the mind" is a kind of a silly idea.

But what if you actually got 20% more brain power just because you wore a certain type of shoe?

Every performance athlete in the world would wanna wear those.

And that’s exactly the point.

These aren't meant to be competition shoes.

These are mental performance footwear to get you primed for the game.

I know it sounds crazy now, but if these actually work, I think Nike just invented a whole new category of footwear.

title: This new immersive tech is going to change sports forever #shorts
description:
This is gonna change sports forever.

What you’re seeing is not a video game.

These are real players in a real stadium.

But the crazy part is…the visuals.

This new tech lets you watch games from any spot on the field.

If I want to sit behind home plate, I can literally create my own camera views.

Imagine watching NFL games as the linebacker or MMA from your favorite fighter’s point of view.

It’ll completely transform the way we watch sports.

Now you’re probably wondering one thing…how is this possible?

Because these shots are obviously not coming from physical cameras.

And that’s the innovation.

This is a brand new type of AI capture technology.

It’s by Arcturus…

…and the way it works is pretty insane.

Arcturus places cameras around the edge of the stadium that all sync together to create a 3D digital twin of the game.

It’s like a clone of the real action, but in the digital world.

And this allows you to pinch, zoom, and view the game from literally anywhere you want in the stadium.

Now of course, this means we’re gonna get reviews and replays from impossible angles.

But it also means….a complete reinvention of sports media.
Any streamer, creator, or sports show will be able to create their own unique broadcasts of the game.

Their own angles, their own sets, on top of their own commentary.

Today, we all get the same few angles from the main networks.

But in the future, this will enable infinite angles from unlimited places on the field.

Now this tech is already available for post-game highlights and is gonna progress to real-time viewing over the next couple years.

The future of sports is gonna look a whole lot different.

title: This new AI headset lets you control your dreams #shorts
description:
This new AI headset lets you control your dreams.

Imagine Inception, but in real life whenever you want.

And they do this by activating lucid dreams.

Now there’s a huge difference between normal dreams and lucid ones.

In normal dreams, this part of your brain is turned off.

It’s called the Dorsal Lateral Prefrontal Cortex.

And that’s where all your decision making happens.

That’s why in normal dreams, weird things happen to you that you can’t control, because your control center is off.

But in lucid dreams, things are completely different.

And this device lets you turn them on whenever you want.

In lucid dreams, you can control your behavior.

You can think, act, build, invent…all in a dream world where there are no risks.

Some of the best inventions in human history came from lucid dreams.

Tesla, Einstein, even the idea for the Periodic Table, came when the inventor was asleep. It’s pretty powerful.

This company Prophetic, created a way to turn on lucid dreaming whenever you want.

It’s called The Halo.

And it fires low intensity ultrasonic energy to automatically activate that part of your brain.

Essentially, waking you up, while you’re sleeping.

Imagine being able to focus your attention during a nap, or trying to experiment with a dangerous solution in a world without danger.

These headsets are gonna be available at the end of this year.

title: This company figured out how to grow shoes from bacteria #shorts
description:
This company figured out how to grow shoes from bacteria.

Yep, it’s as crazy as it sounds.

The entire shoe is made from a single material...PHAs.

Think of PHAs like naturally occuring materials that grow inside of select bacteria.

The company can then harvest the PHAs, turn them into pellets, melt them down, weave them into fiber, and 3D print sustainable shoes and apparel.

Because PHAs are naturally occuring, they are fully sustainable and biodegradable.

The shoes, apparel and other textiles decompose fully with zero carbon footprint.

The project is called O° from Oxman and is the first attempt at creating 100% sustainable clothing.

Traditional shoes are made from 40 different materials and up to 4,000 forever chemicals...O° is made with a single material and zero forever chemicals.

Because of the flexibility of the PHA base unit, Oxman can create a wider range of designs than typically offered by traditional companies.

Would you prefer to wear shoes and apparel if you knew they had zero negative impact on the environment?

title: Schools in China are making students wear AI headbands #shorts
description:
Schools in China are making students wear AI headbands.

And these headbands are tracking everything.

Their focus levels, homework completion, they’re even documenting every time a student yawns.

But here’s the craziest part…

All of that data is being streamed in real-time back to teachers and parents.

And they’re using it to build AI models that improve their curriculum.

It’s pretty insane.

Now the headbands work by showing a light based on a live feed of the student’s brain.

If it’s red, that means the student is deeply focused.

But if it’s blue..that means someone is getting caught in a daydream.

So as students are taking a test, the teacher is live tracking who is paying attention and who isn’t.

And they’re graphing how that attention changes every 10 minutes throughout the entire day.

Those graphs are automatically sent to all the parents.

Imagine daily report cards based only on if you were paying attention.

Pretty crazy

Now the headbands aren’t the only AI tracking tool used at the schools.

They have robots and cameras that are constantly monitoring focus through advanced facial scanning.

And we’re being honest, this is scary, but it might be just the beginning.

AI in education is only gonna wilder, and this is the canary in the coal mine.

Let’s just say…I’m glad they didn’t have these at my school.

title: This is the most insane tech company in the world #shorts
description:
This is the most insane tech company in the world.

ASML.

Every single computer chip on Earth is made using their machines.

Nvidia, Apple, Intel, Samsung…none of them can make any chips without ASML.

But here’s the craziest part…

The way these machines work is completely insane.

If you like tech, this is gonna blow your mind.

This is how computer chips are actually made…

First, the ASML machine fires tiny droplets of molten tin.

50,000 droplets per second…each one thinner than a human hair.

They then shoot a laser at each individual droplet, which vaporizes the tin into a plasma.

That plasma…is hotter than the surface of the sun.

It’s so hot, that when the plasma is created, it also emits extreme UV light.

The machine guides that light through a series of hyper precise mirrors to beam it on a stencil which has the pattern for the chip.

That pattern is then projected onto a thin slice of silicon that’s coated with special light reactive chemicals…essentially using the light to print the pattern onto the silicon.

The machine then repeats this light printing process thousands of times, to build layer upon layer of silicon that stacks together to create a fully functional chip.

Now here’s where it gets wild…

These chip designs are super intricate…billions of transistors spaced only a few atoms apart.

And that means the accuracy of the ASML machines are the equivalent of shooting a laser from the moon and hitting a penny on Earth.

How insane is that?

So in layman’s terms, the process to make a computer chip is vaporizing molten tin to create invisible light that beams atomic patterns onto thousands of layers of silicon.

Shoutout science.

title: This company is building an AI version of Netflix #shorts
description:
This company is building an AI version of Netflix.

It looks like Netflix, but all the shows are made with AI.

And here’s the crazy part…

These aren’t just movies and TV shows that you watch….

They’re actually interactive…where you as the viewer can become a character and even generate your own episodes.

This company’s called Fable.

And it turns out, they might have just invented a whole new category of entertainment.

Now 2 years ago, Fable went super viral for recreating South Park with AI.

You could just type in the plot for any SouthPark episode, and boom, they could generate it.

The characters, the sounds, the scenes, everything…it was pretty incredible.

Now the South Park stunt was just a demo…but it was also the seed for something much bigger.

Playable shows.

And this is where things gets wild.

On Fable’s Showrunner platform, new shows aren’t just episodes that you watch, they’re more like worlds that get created for viewers to build on top of.

Each show has a unique design aesthetic, and things like characters, environments, locations, and plots.

But here’s the difference…

Viewers can dive into the show’s world and make their own episodes.

They can add themselves as characters, turn real locations into scenes, and even come up with their own alternate endings.

And this means every show has a base world with hero episodes but then also thousands of spin-offs, sequels, and fan versions…all combining together to build out the cinematic universe.

And here’s the cool part…

For all those fan spinoffs, there is a revenue split back to the original creator, the one that had dreamed up the foundation for the world in the beginning.

So for example, let’s say you created the original cinematic world of Game of Thrones.

And your small team produced the first couple dozen episodes.

If you built it on Fable, millions of fans around the world could then build on top of your base IP.

And how great would that have been to get alternate endings of the real Game of Thrones?

Now it is still super early days for this technology, but Amazon just invested in Fable, and so it looks like this is gonna be a thing.

I think Fable just pioneered a whole new category of playable content.

They also know, this AI genie is not going back in the bottle.

title: Meta just invented digital fingers #shorts
description:
Meta just invented artificial fingers.

It’s called Digit 360.

That little blue light is a super advanced digital fingertip.

So when it touches an item, it can sense and react just like a human would.

For example, if it touches something prickly, it can recoil.

Or if it picks up something delicate, it can understand and adjust its pressure.

It’s pretty nuts…

But here’s one of the craziest use cases…

Digital fashion.

In the future, brands could take a version of Digit 360, run it across their clothing, and then transmit the feeling of that material digitally.

Because the sensor can read up to 18 different attributes.

Things like texture, material, thread count, density.

So you could be at home, wearing mixed reality glasses….see what the piece looks like, and then if you were wearing haptic gloves, you could literally reach out, touch the piece, and feel the fabric.

All from your couch.

This would create an entirely new virtual shopping category…where brands could build out unique experience in the digital world.

Now this is a bit of a futuristic example, but you get the point.

Digit 360 could also give incredible functionality to prosthetics.

If someone has these on the end of an artificial arm or leg, in theory, it could send more accurate signals through their nervous system about what they’re feeling.

Meta’s working to scale manufacturing on this right now.

And I bet, this will create an entirely new category of embodied experiences.

description:

The reason everyone is freaking out is because Tilly Norwood is the first AI actress ever to be courted by real Hollywood talent agencies.

They’re trying to sign her.

And if she’s signed, that means she could start showing up in movies, TV shows, and tons of other placements alongside real actors and actresses.

Now of course, this has caused insane backlash, tons of debate, and outrage all over Hollywood.

But to me, that’s not even the most interesting part…

The most interesting part is what this really means for the future of AI talent and creativity.

The lines are definitely starting to get blurry between IP goldmines like Lola Rabbit, AI characters like Tilly Norwood, and real actresses like Scarlett Johansson.

Because the truth is, trust and fandom accrues to personalities regardless whether they’re real or fake.

Look at Bugs Bunny, R2D2, even Lil Miquela.

What we’re really watching are the walls around the “garden” of classical creativity come tumbling down.

And this is the real reason why Hollywood is reacting so strongly.

They're not upset…they're scared.

Because at the same time as the moats around production go zero, so are the ones around on-screen talent and distribution.

This is the first time where it actually seems plausible that someone can make a hit film in their bedroom and fans might prefer it over the real thing.

One day will be the first day where an AI character becomes the most famous personality in the world.

And that day might not be today with Tilly Norwood, but it’s coming soon.

title: These hidden easter eggs are super rare in the tech world #shorts
description:
These drawings are super rare in the tech world.

They’re called silicon doodles.

Each one is a tiny graphic that was secretly etched into an old computer chip from the 1980s.

And here’s why they’re so rare…

These drawings are smaller than a spec of dust…it takes a high-powered microscope just to be able to find them on the chip.

They’re like the original easter eggs of Silicon Valley.

But here’s the craziest part of the whole story….

The reason they exist in the first is because back in the day chipmakers used them as a legal defense in court…to prove when competitors copied their designs.

It was like a watermark that nobody knew was there.

So if a cloned chip had the doodle on it, the original creator could prove the designs were stolen.

Pretty interesting.

Now today, collectors all over the world are on a treasure hunt to find the silicon doodles before all the old chips are recycled or destroyed.

There’s even a community and site called Silicon Zoo where fans share and post the best ones they find.

But here’s the funniest part of the whole story…

Turns out, there was one design that seemed to be most popular across all the chips ever created.

1980s computer chip designers…have a guess…here it is.

The playboy bunny.

title: This AI lets you restore old Renaissance paintings #shorts
description:
This guy figured out how to restore old renaissance paintings using AI.

And it works up to 70x faster than traditional methods.

Here's how it works...

First, the AI does a full scan of the original painting.

And based on that scan, it can identify all the gaps and imperfections that are missing.

But then, here’s the cool part…

The AI designs a stack of super thin masks that combine together to recreate the exact colors and shapes the original work needed.

When combined with the original painting, this mask layer gives the experience of instant restoration.

Now the reason this is such a big deal is because 70% of paintings are stuck in storage.

They’re too damaged to show and too expensive for traditional art restorers to fix.

This process uses the same restoration grade materials, but allows classic works to be restored in hours vs months or years.

It's possible that this method could help revive many of the classic works that were permanently destroyed.

Shoutout to Alex, the MIT engineering student that invented this process.

title: Meta just dropped a brand new video tool (massively underrated) #shorts
description:
Meta just dropped a brand new video tool (massively underrated)

It’s called SAM 3.

This model can find any object in any video immediately.

So if I say, where are the zebras?

It knows what a zebra is, it can find them, and I can edit them all…automatically.

And today, that alone is super valuable for tons of video use cases.

You can blur logos in one click, you can highlight and emphasize any subject,

You could even feed it a full NBA game, it could find you every dunk in just a few seconds.

But…the real magic of this has nothing to do with video editing.

Because in the future, this is gonna become a secret weapon for brands.

Imagine being able to use this to find every piece of UGC content where your product shows up, and save those as clips automatically.

Or what about analyzing what other products commonly show up next to yours in different videos?

Eventually, every Meta ad is gonna have this as a processing layer in the backend.

And it will create a whole new medium for real-time video analysis.

Now SAM 3 just launched and it's already live inside Instagram, Facebook Marketplace, and Meta's new Edits app.

But this is just the beginning.

I think this is sneakily one of the most insane launches of 2025 and it’s going to completely change how creators and brands make content.

title: This is the most slept on way to use AI creative tools #shorts
description:
This might be the most slept-on way to use AI creative tools.

It’s called Spaces by Freepik.

Spaces is a node based canvas.

So you can connect text, image, and video nodes together to build a visual AI workflow.

For example, let’s say I wanna make the perfect image of Michael Jordan dunking over a Lamborghini.

Before, I’d have to type a text prompt and manually generate 10 different versions, while tweaking the style description every time.

But Spaces lets me set up a workflow with 10 different branches that automatically runs this process for me.

You can see, I click once, and automatically have 10 different image outputs from all the best image models.

When you rig up multiple of these chains together, you basically create an entirely automated system for generating visuals.

All we have to do is drop in a single line of text from our script, and this workflow spits out 5 AI generated videos, in our desired style, that are all ready to go in just a few minutes.

This type of workflow was super difficult to build before, but with Spaces it’s much much easier.

And the beauty of these workflows is that once you build them, you can share a link so that any other users, like your editors, can redownload the entire workflow on their side, in a single click.

I think these visual canvases are going to be the future of how people use AI creative tools.

Because they make the process feel less like work and more like a video game.

If you wanna try this out, check out Spaces on Freepik.

title: This software turned home design into a video game #shorts
description:
This software turned home design into a video game #shorts It’s called Zuru.

It’s called Zuru.

Now on the platform, you can literally drag and drop any component you’d want in your home.

Walls, doors, windows, archways, high ceilings, staircases…

Everything is completely customizable.

And it feels like a video game, because they literally built the entire platform on top of the same game engine as Fortnite.

But here’s really why this is so powerful and unique…

Because the software is built with AI, all of your design choices are automatically pressure tested for real building codes.

The structural support, compliance, energy efficiency, weather patterns…it’s all automatically checked and incorporated while you’re designing.

And the plumbing, the lightswitches, the electrical…they’re all automatically laid in a single click.

This means literally anyone, even a 5 year old, could design a real home that could actually be built safely, anywhere in the world.

But the thing is…the design alone is only half the story.

Because on Zuru, once you design the home you want, you can just press print, and their robot factory will build and assemble the entire thing for you.

And this was the part that really blew my mind.

For every home or building, Zuru creates a digital twin of every single component and then feeds it into their fully autonomous factory.

First they print a mini-home at 1/4th the scale to test and make sure everything works.

Then, they feed the validated schematics into their megafactory that literally prints the entire building without any human intervention.

By automating the manufacturing, they’re bringing the cost of building down significantly…

People are gonna be able to build homes for 75% less than normal prices.

It’s gonna be wild

And get this…

Zuru bought up a bunch of the Malibu property that was for sale from the wildfires…

And so their next project…is to use this technology…to rebuild the California coast.

title: OpenAI just launched their own social platform #shorts
description:
OpenAI just launched their own social platform.

It’s called Sora 2.

And this is officially the moment where the AI race got interesting.

In the new app, you can train an AI avatar of yourself, and then use it to make AI generated videos.

So think of it like a brand new social feed, but with only AI-generated content.

Now I know what you’re thinking...

We do not need another social feed.
 
And we definitely don’t need another one with AI slop content.

And I agree, but there are some pretty interesting features here.

For one, the memeability in this app is gonna be insane.

You’re gonna be able to collab with celebrities that open up their likeness to the public.

So if Drake lets me, I can put him and I in our own version of Oceans 11, and the quality of the videos is actually pretty insane.

You’re gonna see actors doing skits with athletes, celebrities cross posting with musicians, it’s gonna be wild to see how this evolves.

I predict this AI social app will become a brand new surface for creativity, and give birth to an entire new genre of creators.

But the reason this launch is so interesting, actually has nothing to do with the features in the app.

The reason it’s so interesting is because this launch represents a trillion dollar 4D chess game that’s playing out in real-time.

Every tech company is trying to build a full stack strategy for AI.

They want to own the consumer hardware layer, the AI models, the applications, and most importantly, the social distribution.

It’s not enough to just have an AI model like ChatGPT, you also need to control the consumer hardware it’s used on, and have a social asset to drive new users to it.

So what you’re really witnessing with the launch of this app, is a shot across the bow by OpenAI as they jostle for tech positioning.

OpenAI already has the world’s best AI models and applications, but they have no consumer hardware device or social distribution.

Well, they just acquired Jony Ive to build the hardware, and Sora 2 is their distribution.

What you’re watching is an all out business war at a scale we’ve never seen. 

And OpenAI just made a huge move.

title: This tool lets you turn a single image into a complete cinematic world #shorts
description:
You can now turn a single image into a complete cinematic world.

If you make content, this a huge hack.

It’s called Angles by Freepik.

Start by taking any image and drop it into Freepik Spaces.

You can then add a Camera Angle node and specify how you want the angle to change.

Just drag the rotate, vertical and closeup sliders.

This creates an angle preset.

And with just one click you can convert your base image into a new angle, with the exact same character and scene.

But that’s just level 1.

Because with Spaces, we can then build out 10 different camera angle nodes that all run at the same time.

All we have to do is press go on the starting image, and we get 10 different angles of the exact same character and world, automatically.

Now this is super valuable for 2 reasons.

For one, if you wanna make a video of the same subject with continuous motion, you can use these new angle shots as starting and ending frames.

And if you string them together, you get something like this.

It is almost impossible to control motion in AI video better than this.

But the cooler part is the speed you unlock at world building.

Most creative storytelling requires character consistency.

If you can’t hold the character constant, it just doesn’t sell the story as well.

This workflow solves for character consistency because you’re building a world library of different shots to use as starting points.

If you wanna try this out, checkout Freepik and use the Angle node in Spaces.

title: This company uses AI-powered drones to clean the ocean #shorts
description:
This company uses AI-powered drones to clean the ocean.

It’s called CircularBlue.

And the way this thing works is insane.

Now the goal of this device is to pick up floating plastic and trash in the water. 

To find the plastic, the mothership sends out autonomous AI-powered scout drones that scan the water from above.

When they locate something, the hub dispatches a GPS-guided vessel to go and collect it.

And this process happens over and over all throughout the coastline.

The entire system is fully autonomous, solar powered, and uses sea water to propel itself.

So in theory, this machine could run forever…it’s pretty cool.

But the most interesting part of this story isn’t the technology itself…

The most interesting part is who created it.

It turns out, CircularBlue was invented by Rhinoshield…a Taiwanese company that manufactures phone cases.

And it sounds weird at first, but if you think about it, this is a genius business move.

Rhinoshield is using the collected ocean plastic to manufacture new phone cases from recycled materials.

They call this fully circular production.

And it’s genius because they get a triple benefit.

Great PR on the initiative, a positive help to the world, and free raw materials to keep producing new products.  

This first prototype was a $2M investment into the future of ocean sustainability.

And as it continues to work, they plan to scale this effort all around the world.

title: This is the future of product marketing #shorts
description:
This is the future of product marketing.

You can now make product animations like this, super easily with AI.

Here’s how to do it…

Go to Freepik, select Nano Banana as the image model and then drop in any photo of your product.

You can then use simple prompts to generate other photos of the product at different angles.

Side shots, close ups, top down…you can get really creative with this.

Personally I think the exploding effect really makes this whole thing work, so be sure to throw that in there as well.

Once you have 7-10 different angles as photos, switch to the Video Generator in Freepik and use Kling 2.1.

You can pick any two of your images as the first and last frame and then describe the desired motion you want for the transition.

You don’t need to get too crazy here.

Zoom in, zoom out, rotation, that’s all you need.

Once you have these video clips, all you need is a little sauce in Premiere Pro on the speed ramping as you connect them together and you end up with something like this.

Just a couple years ago, this type of product animation video would have cost $20,000 and taken multiple weeks to make.

Now literally anyone on your marketing team can crank these out for $5 in a couple hours.

title: This new AI device helps kids learn super fast #shorts
description:
This is a brand new AI device built specifically to help kids learn.

It’s called Dex.

And I think this is a glimpse into the future of AI learning.

Now the way Dex works is super simple.

Kids point it at any object and immediately take a photo.

The Dex AI will recognize the object, explain what it is, and translate it into any language that kid is learning.

So it’s kind of like Duolingo meets a personal tutor, but built for kids and always available.

This helps get kids off of the iPad and into the physical world to explore more freely.

Now the device was built by 3 parents that were worried about their kids' increasing screen time.

But as they were building it, they came across a huge problem…

How do you build an AI tool that’s safe for 3 year olds to use?

Dex has super strict safety prompts as well as a zero data retention policy.

So it doesn’t actually store or save any images as they’re captured.

Parents can also monitor progress in a dedicated side app, which is super helpful to track developful.

But the most interesting part about this isn’t the hardware itself…

It’s the case study for how learning with AI is starting to evolve.

Every device in your life is going to get smarter.

They’re all gonna get cameras, they’re all gonna have AI brains, and they’re all going to get hyper personalized to you after just a few minutes of use.

Dex is the first great example of AI learning hardware built for kids.

And I think, this is just the beginning.

title: Here's how to use AI to build J.A.R.V.I.S. #shorts
description:
You know JARVIS from Ironman?

What if I told you…we’re actually way closer to building it, than people realize.

To make JARVIS, all you need is an AI system that can do 5 things…

First, you have to be able to communicate with it.

It needs to hear you clearly and speak back fluidly.

But we already have AI tools that can do this really well.

Eleven Labs and Hume AI are world class voice models that are already at Jarvis’s level.

So that listening and talking piece, is kinda good to go.

Second, JARVIS needs to be able to see things and visually understand what’s going on.

It can’t just have ears, it also, needs to have eyes.

And those eyes wouldn’t be through a single humanoid, it would be through a series of cameras all over your space.

But again, we already have world action models that are getting pretty good at seeing and understanding.

They’re still in the early stages, but Jepa 2 from Meta, Gemini 2.0, Tesla, Figure,…these are at the core for how robots are being trained today.

So JARVIS’s eyesight capabilities are also a lot further along than people realize.

But the third piece is the biggest one…

In order for Jarvis to work, you need one central AI brain that can essentially hop around freely to different robotics and smart devices throughout your space.

It’s kinda like seamlessly switching sonos speakers from room to room, but on steroids.

Today our smart devices are actually pretty dumb.

So to have a legit working version of Jarvis, we’d need an interconnected system of devices and machines that is smart enough to activate as you maneuver around it.

So that’s the brain and body…still a ways to go.

Part 4 and 5 is really what makes JARVIS, JARVIS.

And that’s the customizable personality and the proactive action system.

On the personality front, we really just need better memory and emotional expression.

But this is also coming and the progress in memory is obvious in the latest versions of ChatGPT.

The proactive action system is having JARVIS assess and be able to do stuff without you asking.

This is essentially an AI agent system.

And again, companies are starting to lay the rails for proactive agents.

So what JARVIS really does… is use eyesight to understand what’s going on, proactively take action using an interconnected hardware network, and then communicate what it’s doing back to you in a personalized and entertaining way.

Ironically the biggest problem holding back JARVIS today is not that our AI isn’t smart enough…

It’s that our cameras and robots are too dumb….for now

title: This AI worldbuilding platform will change marketing #shorts
description:
This new worldbuilding technology is going to change marketing.

You can now use AI to put yourself in a 3D world.

You can control the scene, the motion, the movement, you can even lipsync the audio to match any vocal reference you’ve recorded.

If you wanna do this for yourself, these are the tools I used.

And shoutout Rourke, he’s the OG for this.

First you need spatial reference photos of your world.

You can use World Labs, Genie 3, or even a VR headset.

We use World Labs cause they have a bunch of different preset environments you can pick from for free.

But also, you can upload any photo and it will turn it into a 3D spatial world for you.

When you’re in the world, you want to take screenshots from a bunch of different perspectives.

I like to think of these as sets, that you can build on top of.

Once you have shots of the sets, splice yourself into the scenes using Google’s Nano Banana image model.

We use Freepik to do this and it’s super super easy.

When you have the combined image, you can take these into an AI video generator like Veo 3 or Sora 2 to bring them to life.

And here’s the sauce layer on top…

If you want to lipsync so your character talks smoothly you can use Wan 2.2, Infinite Talk, or the new Veo 3.1 video model.

And that’s the whole stack.

Now to me, this workflow is the canary in the coal mine for where marketing is going.

If you’re a clothing brand, you’re gonna be able to build a world that becomes a collection of sets for your fans to make content in.

My favorite brands are Kith and Represent, so we just took lifestyle photos from their recent shoots and turned them into playable, immersive environments.

We are just in the early innings of what synthetic experiential marketing is gonna look like.

And I’m telling you, worldbuilding is the next frontier.
