title: This new AI video model can think creatively on its own #shorts
description:
This is a brand new AI video model that can think creatively on its own.

It doesn’t just make videos from prompts, it can actually reason through creative decisions before it generates anything.

This is Ray3 from Luma AI.

And I’m telling you, this is the new state of the art for AI video.

Here’s why the reasoning is so impressive.

Let’s say you have a starting image of a woman, and you prompt “have the woman look down, show two vintage phones, and then have her pick up the blue one.”

Now most video models would try to generate this scene.

And maybe you get it perfect on the first try, but usually not.

Typically you’re stuck generating 20 different videos over half an hour, hoping to get lucky with the slot machine pull.

But Ray3 works completely differently.

Ray3 also generates the first video, but then it can think back to the original context and use creative logic and reasoning to grade the output.

It can detect when the clip doesn’t make sense creatively or cinematically, figure out what to fix, and will regenerate it again on its own.

It runs this logic loop over and over until it’s happy with a shot that matches your original intention.

And does that all automatically for you, which is pretty crazy.

If you make videos, this is kinda like you have an editor or director embedded into the AI by default.

The bar for AI video, has just been raised again.

title: The best new workflow for editing images with AI in Adobe Photoshop #shorts
description:
This is the best new workflow for editing images with AI.

It’s @photoshop and their two new AI partner models…Nano Banana Pro and Flux Kontext Pro.

And I partnered with Adobe Photoshop because if you can learn this one thing, you can make pretty much anything when it comes to AI image generation and editing.

Let’s say you have this base image and you’re looking to change the text on that neon sign.

You can just highlight the sign, and use Generative Fill to prompt the new text you want it to say.

But here’s the big question…

When you see the list of AI models in Generative Fill, you’ll notice there is both Nano Banana Pro and Flux Kontext Pro.

So which one do you use?

In this case, Nano Banana Pro is absolutely insane with multilingual text rendering and subject consistency.

So it is the perfect choice to solve this neon sign text switch.

But on the other hand, if you’re trying to change individual elements in a scene that require consistent lighting and perspective, you’d wanna try using Flux Kontext Pro.

It’s great when things like lighting, environment, or individual elements need to stay cohesively blended in a scene.

The easy way to think of it is like this…

Subtle contextual changes, especially lighting and environmental, is Flux Kontext Pro.

Major stylistic changes, subject consistency, and text rendering is Nano Banana Pro.

But the cool part is, because they’re both available with a single click, you can just mix and match in any scenario to compare and learn which is best.

If you’re a designer, marketer, or entrepreneur that needs to use visual imagery to tell stories, using these tools will speed things up 10x.

You can try out Photoshop with Nano Banana Pro and Flux Kontext Pro today!

title: People are paying $20,000 for these robots to do their chores #shorts
description:
We have officially entered the era of the in-home robot.

And it feels like science fiction, but people are actually paying $20,000 to buy these robots for their home.

This is called Neo from 1X.

It’s like C3PO meets a teletubby, that can be programmed to do chores in your house.

Now right away you'll notice, Neo looks different from most of the other robots we’ve been seeing.

And that’s because it was intentionally designed to look softer, safer, and more humanlike.

And it makes sense…

If at-home robots are gonna be a thing, they’ll probably look closer to something like this vs a huge metallic droid…you know, so they don’t terrify your two year old.

Now I’ll admit, the demo video for Neo was pretty insane.

It allows you to schedule a list of chores, leave the house, and then come back to them completely done.

And just like a baby monitor, you can tap into the live camera feed from Neo’s eyes whenever you want, so you can make sure your house is hopefully still standing by the time you get home.

Now to be honest, I can’t tell if this is the most helpful productivity hack ever invented, or the prologue of a Black Mirror episode that’s gonna go terribly wrong.

But either way, the most interesting part about this whole story isn’t really Neo’s design or abilities.

The most interesting part is this thing called Expert Mode.

While Neo can do common chores on its own, if you want it to do something more unique or specialized, you’re gonna have to have a human teleoperate the robot.

That means there’s literally gonna be a warehouse of human workers controlling robots in your home like a video game.

Pretty wild.

And here’s why they’re doing that…

In order to scale robots, in any application, these companies need millions and millions of real-life training scenarios.

And the only way to get them is to put real robots in real homes, and see what happens.

So in a way, these early adopters buying the robots are the guinea pigs that are training the robot hive mind.

And if that doesn’t sound Black Mirror, I don’t know what will.

title: We officially just entered the era of the worldbuilder #shorts

For example, in this demo, the creator is iteratively building a scene in Manhattan.

But the coolest part is when he says, “Now let’s make this come alive” and the people and cars start moving inside the world he just created.

It’s kind of like GTA or Sims but being built and iterated in real-time.

We are about to enter an entirely new era of visual expression.

One where anyone can build worlds like this from scratch with just their voice.

Think Tony Stark talking to Jarvis, but to build entire digital environments that you can play and work inside of.

And this is being made possible by AI tools like Genie 3 from Google, Runway ML and Veo 3.

In my mind, AI world models are gonna change everything about the way fans interact with their favorite projects.

Musicians and artists will be able to invent a whole new category of spaces to consume their art.

Brands will be able to build immersive worlds for their biggest fans.

Imagine if your favorite clothing brand could build their version of a digital Disney World from scratch in just a few days?

It’s all coming and will be made possible by AI world models.

If you’re a creator, artist, entrepreneur or brand…strap in.

Because we’ve officially just entered the era of the worldbuilder.

title: This company uses AI to find buried treasure #shorts
tags: AI found treasure|AI found gold|robot treasure hunting|AI gold rush|future treasure hunting|AI treasure map|AI in mining|AI found lithium|AI discovered platinum|AI found silver|AI changed exploration|hidden treasure AI|future of gold mining|AI digging gold|AI space treasure|Earth AI discovery|AI found rare minerals|AI future tech|AI science breakthrough|AI changed the world|tech|ai
description:
This company is pioneering the future of treasure hunting.

They used AI to find literal gold buried inside the Earth.

And here’s the crazy part…

The way they found the gold was super unique.

They gave maps to AI and let the AI tell them where the gold was buried.

And believe it or not, that actually worked.

The company’s called Earth AI.

And what they figured out, might have just changed exploration forever.

Earth AI had a really simple idea

They took 50 years worth of geological data and fed it into a special AI model.

The AI used this data to identify hot zones on the map where rare minerals might be.

They call this their Mineral Targeting Platform.

Now once the team gets to those hot zones, they use next generation drilling to sample the mineral deposits.

And here’s the wild part.

It turns out, this AI system is amazing at finding the best spots where the minerals are buried.

In 2024, companies around the world spent $280 million dollars per successful hot zone.

Earth AI, spends just $700K.

That’s 400x cheaper.

And they’ve found everything from gold and silver, to platinum and lithium.
It’s pretty insane.

But I think this is just the beginning…

In a few years, we’ll have AI models that can run this process all over the world.

Finding buried treasure on Earth and in space.

title: 3 easy hacks to fix your phone addiction #shorts
description:
If you want to be less addicted to your phone, here are 3 simple hacks that will help.

This took my screen time 12 hours down to 3 hours.

First thing is called the Brick.

The Brick acts like a physical key that locks you out of certain apps.

Once you Brick your phone, the only way to unbrick it is to physically touch this device back to your phone to unlock it again.

This means you can brick your social media apps, take your phone with you, and leave the physical Brick key behind.

Even if you wanted to go on social media you couldn’t, because you’re Bricked.

I love this thing.

Tip #2 is the greyscale triple click.

If I triple click my side power button, my phone immediately goes greyscale.

And this is huge…because as soon as the colors are gone from your phone, it becomes 10x less interesting to look at.

The way to set this up is to go to Settings - Accessibility - scroll down to Accessibility Shortcut - and select Color Filters.

Any time you triple click the side button, the greyscale will activate. You can always reset the colors but triple clicking again.

Tip #3 is to leave your phone in another room when you go to bed.

If your doomscrolling is the last thing you do before bed and the first thing you do when you wake up, this is what’s killing your mental health.

I know it, because I was doing it too.

Leave your phone plugged in another room at night, and your life will get a million times better.

If you need an alarm, just buy a 10 dollar alarm on amazon.

If you leave a bricked phone in another room, you’ll be cured of phone addiction in 14 days. It’s magic.

title: If you're worried about AI taking your job, you should read this #shorts
description:
If you’re worried about your job being replaced by AI, you should read this…

It’s a study by Microsoft that analyzes the top 40 careers that are most and least at risk.

Some of the jobs most at risk include translators, historians, sales reps, and writers.

Some of the jobs least at risk include nurses, roofers, builders and dishwashers.

Now nobody knows for sure what’s gonna happen.

But the reason this is so interesting, is because this study was run differently than all of the others in this category.

Instead of guessing and playing “what if,” Microsoft analyzed all the daily work tasks that are already being replaced by AI today.

And they used those tasks to form a score predicting what percentage of each job could be done by AI in the future.

Now, if you wanna read the full report, comment AI and I'll send it to you

But the big takeaway is this…

The jobs that everyone thought were “safe” are actually the most at-risk.

Blue collar physical jobs are basically AI-proof.

While white collar and creative jobs are being completely disrupted.

Either way, my goal is to help everybody figure out how to navigate this AI era.

But one thing’s for sure, we’re about to enter a whole new world.
